children.heading: Development of core set of health care quality measures for adults eligible for benefits under Medicaid
children.label: a Development of core set of health care quality measures for adults eligible for benefits under Medicaid
children.text: The Secretary shall identify and publish a recommended core set of adult health quality measures for Medicaid eligible adults in the same manner as the Secretary identifies and publishes a core set of child health quality measures under , including with respect to identifying and publishing existing adult health quality measures that are in use under public and privately sponsored health care coverage arrangements, or that are part of reporting systems that measure both the presence and duration of health insurance coverage over time, that may be applicable to Medicaid eligible adults.section 1320b–9a of this title

children.heading: Recommended measures
children.label: 1 Recommended measures
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall identify and publish for comment a recommended core set of adult health quality measures for Medicaid eligible adults.January 1, 2011

children.heading: Dissemination
children.label: 2 Dissemination
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall publish an initial core set of adult health quality measures that are applicable to Medicaid eligible adults.January 1, 2012

children.heading: Voluntary reporting
children.label: A Voluntary reporting
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary, in consultation with States, shall develop a standardized format for reporting information based on the initial core set of adult health quality measures and create procedures to encourage States to use such measures to voluntarily report information regarding the quality of health care for Medicaid eligible adults.January 1, 2013

children.heading: Mandatory reporting with respect to behavioral health measures
children.label: B Mandatory reporting with respect to behavioral health measures
children.text: Beginning with the State report required under subsection (d)(1) for 2024, the Secretary shall require States to use all behavioral health measures included in the core set of adult health quality measures and any updates or changes to such measures to report information, using the standardized format for reporting information and procedures developed under subparagraph (A), regarding the quality of behavioral health care for Medicaid eligible adults.

children.heading: Reports to Congress
children.label: 4 Reports to Congress
children.text: Not later than , and every 3 years thereafter, the Secretary shall include in the report to Congress required under information similar to the information required under that section with respect to the measures established under this section.January 1, 2014section 1320b–9a(a)(6) of this title

children.heading: Establishment of Medicaid quality measurement program
children.label: 5 Establishment of Medicaid quality measurement program

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 12 months after the release of the recommended core set of adult health quality measures under paragraph (1)) , the Secretary shall establish a Medicaid Quality Measurement Program in the same manner as the Secretary establishes the pediatric quality measures program under .1section 1320b–9a(b) of this titleSo in original. The second closing parenthesis probably should not appear.1

children.heading: Revising, strengthening, and improving initial core measures
children.label: B Revising, strengthening, and improving initial core measures
children.text: Beginning not later than 24 months after the establishment of the Medicaid Quality Measurement Program, and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall publish recommended changes to the initial core set of adult health quality measures that shall reflect the results of the testing, validation, and consensus process for the development of adult health quality measures.

children.heading: Behavioral health measures
children.label: C Behavioral health measures
children.text: Beginning with respect to State reports required under subsection (d)(1) for 2024, the core set of adult health quality measures maintained under this paragraph (and any updates or changes to such measures) shall include behavioral health measures.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: c Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as supporting the restriction of coverage, under subchapter XIX or XXI or otherwise, to only those services that are evidence-based, or in anyway limiting available services.

children.heading: Annual State reports regarding State-specific quality of care measures applied under Medicaid
children.label: d Annual State reports regarding State-specific quality of care measures applied under Medicaid
A
children.text: State-specific adult health quality measures applied by the State under such plan, including measures described in subsection (b)(5) and, beginning with the report for 2024, all behavioral health measures included in the core set of adult health quality measures maintained under such subsection (b)(5) and any updates or changes to such measures (as required under subsection (b)(3)); and

children.label: B
children.text: State-specific information on the quality of health care furnished to Medicaid eligible adults under such plan, including information collected through external quality reviews of managed care organizations under and benchmark plans under .section 1396u–2 of this titlesection 1396u–7 of this title

children.heading: Publication
children.label: 2 Publication
children.text: Not later than , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall collect, analyze, and make publicly available the information reported by States under paragraph (1).September 30, 2014

children.heading: Appropriation
children.label: e Appropriation
children.text: Out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, there is appropriated for each of fiscal years 2010 through 2014, $60,000,000 for the purpose of carrying out this section. Funds appropriated under this subsection shall remain available until expended. Of the funds appropriated under this subsection, not less than $15,000,000 shall be used to carry out .section 1320b–9a(b) of this title
